reasoning: The informal statement says the power series $\sum z^n/n$ converges at every point of the unit circle *except* $z=1$. The natural mathematical reading is a biconditional: the set of points on the unit circle where the series converges is exactly the unit circle minus $\{1\}$ (and indeed at $z=1$ the series becomes the divergent harmonic series). The Lean statement fixes $z$ on the unit circle via $\|z\|=1$, lets $s_n = \sum_{i<n} z^{i+1}/(i+1)$ be the partial sums of $\sum z^n/n$ starting at $n=1$, and asserts convergence of $s_n$ iff $z \ne 1$. The types, quantifier structure, definition of partial sums, and biconditional all match the informal claim.
